latest.title: Director/PDMR Shareholding
latest.content:
19 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 2139X
ActiveOps PLC
19 March 2026
19 March 2026
ActiveOps Plc
(the "Company" or the "Group")
Director/PDMR Dealing
ActiveOps plc (AIM: AOM), a leading provider of Decision Intelligence for service operations
, announces that it has been notified of the following transactions in ordinary shares in the Company undertaken by directors/persons discharging managerial responsibility (PDMR) as a result of their participation in the Company's all-employee Share Incentive Plan.
The notifications below are made in accordance with Article 19(3) of the Market Abuse Regulation:
1
Details of the person discharging managerial responsibilities / person closely associated
a)
Name
1. Richard Jeffery
2
Reason for the notification
a)
Position/status
1. Executive Chair
b)
Initial notification /Amendment
Initial Notification.
3
Details of the issuer, emission allowance market participant, auction platform, auctioneer or auction monitor
a)
Name
ActiveOps plc
b)
LEI
213800RHBFOK6YNI3B30
4
Details of the transaction(s): section to be repeated for (i) each type of instrument; (ii) each type of transaction; (iii) each date; and (iv) each place where transactions have been conducted
a)
Description of the financial instrument, type of instrument
Ordinary shares of 0.01 pence each
Identification code
ISIN Code: GB00BLH37Y17
b)
Nature of the transaction
Acquisition of partnership shares through the Company's Share Incentive Plan
c)
Price(s) and volume(s)
Price(s)
Volume(s)
1. £2.15
1. 71
d)
Aggregated information
- Aggregated volume
N/A
- Price
e)
Date of the transaction
17 March 2026
f)
Place of the transaction
London Stock Exchange
For more information, please contact:
ActiveOps
Via Alma
Richard Jeffery, Executive Chair
www.activeops.com
Emma Salthouse, Chief Financial Officer
Investec Bank plc
+44 (0)20 7597 5970
Nominated Adviser and Joint Broker
Patrick Robb / Nick Prowting / James Smith
Canaccord Genuity Limited
+44 (0)20 7523 8000
Joint Broker
Simon Bridges / Harry Gooden / Harry Rees
Alma Strategic Communications
+ 44(0) 203 405 0205
Caroline Forde / Louisa El-Ahwal
About ActiveOps
ActiveOps is a Software as a Service business, dedicated to helping organisations create more value from their service operations. ActiveOps' Decision Intelligence software solutions are specifically designed to support leaders with the vast number of decisions they make daily in the running their operations. Our customers make better decisions and consume less time and effort making them. The outcomes are significantly improved turnaround times and double-digit improvements in productivity with backlogs of work materially reduced. Customers also leverage the capacity created to invest in transformation and development, and more efficiently utilise resources.
​
The Company's AI-powered SaaS solutions are underpinned by 15+ years of operational data and its AOM methodology which is proven to enhance cross departmental decision-making.
​
The Company has approximately 280 employees, serving a global base of enterprise customers from offices in the UK, Ireland, USA, Canada, Australia, India, and South Africa. The Group's customers are predominantly in the banking, insurance, healthcare administration and business process outsourcing (BPO) sectors, including Nationwide, TD Bank, Elevance and Xchanging.
.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact
rns@lseg.com
or visit
www.rns.com
.RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. For further information about how RNS and the London Stock Exchange use the personal data you provide us, please see our
Privacy Policy
.
END

ActiveOps Plc
(the "Company" or the "Group")
Notification of major holdings
ActiveOps plc (AIM: AOM), a leading provider of Decision Intelligence for service operations
, has been notified by Octopus Investments Limited ("Octopus") of the following changes to its shareholding in ActiveOps. The notifications were submitted by Octopus to the Company on time however, due to an administrative error at the Company, the Company is only now making announcement.

About ActiveOps
ActiveOps is a Software as a Service business, dedicated to helping organisations create more value from their service operations. ActiveOps' Decision Intelligence software solutions are specifically designed to support leaders with the vast number of decisions they make daily in the running their operations. Our customers make better decisions and consume less time and effort making them. The outcomes are significantly improved turnaround times and double-digit improvements in productivity with backlogs of work materially reduced. Customers also leverage the capacity created to invest in transformation and development, and more efficiently utilise resources.
The Company's AI-powered SaaS solutions are underpinned by 15+ years of operational data and its AOM methodology which is proven to enhance cross departmental decision-making.
The Company has approximately 280 employees, serving a global base of enterprise customers from offices in the UK, Ireland, USA, Canada, Australia, India, and South Africa. The Group's customers are predominantly in the banking, insurance, healthcare administration and business process outsourcing (BPO) sectors, including Nationwide, TD Bank, Elevance and Xchanging.

latest.content:

RNS Number : 7781U
ActiveOps PLC
27 February 2026
27 February 2026
ActiveOps Plc
(the "Company" or the "Group")
Total Voting Rights and Share Capital
ActiveOps plc (AIM: AOM), a leading provider of Decision Intelligence for service operations,
makes the following announcement in accordance with Rule 5.6.1 of the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency rules.
As of 27th February 2026, the total number of ordinary shares of £0.001 each in issue is 71,449,528. Therefore, the total number of voting rights in ActiveOps plc is 71,449,528.

ActiveOps

ActiveOps is a Software as a Service business, dedicated to helping organisations create more value from their service operations. Our customers make better decisions and consume less time and effort making them. The outcomes are significantly improved turnaround times and double-digit improvements in productivity with backlogs of work materially reduced. ActiveOps Plc
(the "Company" or the "Group")
Director/PDMR Dealing
ActiveOps plc (AIM: AOM), a leading provider of Decision Intelligence for service operations
, announces that it has been notified of the following transactions in ordinary shares in the Company undertaken by directors/persons discharging managerial responsibility (PDMR) as a result of their participation in the Company's all-employee Share Incentive Plan.
The notifications below are made in accordance with Article 19(3) of the Market Abuse Regulation:
1

For more information, please contact:
ActiveOps
Via Alma
Richard Jeffery, Executive Chair
www.activeops.com
Emma Salthouse, Chief Financial Officer
Investec Bank plc
+44 (0)20 7597 5970
Nominated Adviser and Joint Broker
Patrick Robb / Nick Prowting / James Smith
Canaccord Genuity Limited
+44 (0)20 7523 8000
Joint Broker
Simon Bridges / Harry Gooden / Harry Rees
Alma Strategic Communications
+ 44(0) 203 405 0205
Caroline Forde / Louisa El-Ahwal
About ActiveOps
ActiveOps is a Software as a Service business, dedicated to helping organisations create more value from their service operations. ActiveOps' Decision Intelligence software solutions are specifically designed to support leaders with the vast number of decisions they make daily in the running their operations. Our customers make better decisions and consume less time and effort making them. The outcomes are significantly improved turnaround times and double-digit improvements in productivity with backlogs of work materially reduced. Customers also leverage the capacity created to invest in transformation and development, and more efficiently utilise resources.
​
The Company's AI-powered SaaS solutions are underpinned by 15+ years of operational data and its AOM methodology which is proven to enhance cross departmental decision-making.
​
The Company has approximately 280 employees, serving a global base of enterprise customers from offices in the UK, Ireland, USA, Canada, Australia, India, and South Africa. The Group's customers are predominantly in the banking, insurance, healthcare administration and business process outsourcing (BPO) sectors, including Nationwide, TD Bank, Elevance and Xchanging.
.

company_30s.what_they_do: ActiveOps Ltd delivers cloud‑based workforce management software that helps companies automate scheduling, time‑tracking and compliance for on‑site staff. The platform is sold on a subscription model to mid‑size and larger enterprises in retail, hospitality and industrial sectors.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Workforce automation is a growing market as businesses look to reduce manual labor costs and improve compliance. ActiveOps offers a scalable, SaaS solution that plugs into existing HR and payroll systems, giving it a competitive edge over legacy on‑premise tools. Its customer base includes several well‑known retail chains, giving it a foothold in a high‑volume industry.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in a growth phase, with no clear revenue figures disclosed publicly. Management has been focusing on product development and securing new contracts, while keeping cash burn moderate. The share price is trading far below its historical highs, suggesting the market has not yet recognized the upside.

rns_narrative: A series of shareholder‑related announcements – new director shareholdings, a block listing return, and a contingent share award – signals a restructuring of the capital base. This is often a precursor to a fresh funding round or a strategic partnership.

The company has also issued a notification of major holdings and a grant of contingent share awards, indicating that senior management is aligning its interests with the market and may be preparing for a future listing or a significant equity transaction.
These moves suggest the firm is stabilising its governance and positioning itself for a catalyst that could lift the stock from its current low.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of long‑term holders posted in 2022 praising new product launches and a strong AGM. They see the company as a niche leader that could break out once the market recognises its value.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: A few voices criticised the lack of a clear website and the absence of recent earnings data, warning that the business may struggle to generate cash flow.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has faded dramatically since 2022; the only recent posts are neutral or mildly bullish, indicating the crowd is largely indifferent. This silence creates a contrarian opportunity.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – there is no public buzz. A sudden announcement could therefore produce a large surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming AGM in September could reveal a new revenue‑generation plan or a strategic partnership that would justify a price rally.
A potential funding round or equity offering announced in the next 2‑3 months would bring fresh capital and validate management’s plan.
A quarterly earnings release that shows a positive trend in subscription revenue or customer acquisition would confirm the business model.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike of at least 2–3× the 10‑day average coupled with a break above the recent 20‑day moving average. A clear support level around the current trading range that holds for at least 5 consecutive days would signal that the crash bottom has been reached.
expect_next.timing_regime: The APEX score indicates a tight compression, suggesting the price is still in the early stages of a recovery. The market has likely exhausted its negative bias, and a mean‑reversion move is plausible once a catalyst triggers.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Deep crashes historically produce the largest rebounds – the system’s RED and GREEN signals show that a 70‑95% drop often returns 40‑60% of the lost value and sometimes more. If the stock climbs to the midpoint of its 52‑week range, that would represent roughly a 200% upside from today’s level.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Without a clear earnings story, the company could remain a cash‑burning entity. A delisting risk or an inability to secure new contracts would cap upside. The worst case is that the price stays near its current trough and the market keeps discounting the stock.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 200‑300% if the price rebounds to mid‑range levels. Downside: limited to the current price level – you can’t lose more than you invest. The asymmetry is favourable for a contrarian position.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to the 20‑day moving average or a key support level within the current range. Size the position so that a 10% drop from entry would trigger a stop. Use a trailing stop that follows the price by 8% once the stock moves up 12% from entry.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume surge of at least twice the 10‑day average and a clear break above the 20‑day moving average. Alternatively, a positive earnings release or a partnership announcement would be a trigger.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a bankruptcy filing, a regulatory investigation, or a forced delisting, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep‑crashed, silent stock that may rebound sharply once a new funding round or partnership is announced – a contrarian play with high upside and limited downside.
